# Welcome {.unnumbered}

::: {.hero-book}
::: {.hero-book__media}
![DATA-R 155 course banner for Introduction to R Programming](assets/images/data-r-155-banner.png){.hero-book__banner fig-alt="Data R 155 Introduction to R Programming banner."}
:::

::: {.hero-book__content}
<div class="hero-book__eyebrow">DATA-R 155 • Boise State University</div>

## Guided activities for Introduction to R Programming

This book contains the guided activities for **Introduction to R Programming**. It is designed to help you move between reading instructions, typing code in RStudio, checking what happened, and reflecting on what you are learning.

<div class="chip-row">
  <span class="course-chip">Seven-week online course</span>
  <span class="course-chip">Guided activities</span>
  <span class="course-chip">Code-along learning</span>
</div>

<div class="book-actions">
  <a class="cta-primary" href="front-matter/preface.qmd">Start with the Preface</a>
  <a class="cta-secondary" href="chapters/01-tenacity/ch01-navigation.Rmd">Open Chapter 1</a>
</div>
:::
:::

::: {.home-note}
You are not expected to know this before you begin. The goal of this book is not perfect code on the first try. The goal is to build a reliable workflow for setting a goal, typing code, checking results, and revising your thinking.
:::

## What this book is

This is the guided activity book for the course. Each chapter supports your learning by walking you through explanations, screenshots, code you type in RStudio, checks, summaries, chapter terms, and practice connections.

## What this book is not

This book is not the gradebook, the discussion board, or the submission portal. Use the guided activities to learn and review. Use Canvas and course assignment directions for submission, grading, and progress tracking.

## How to use the guided activities

Use the book with RStudio open beside it.

- Read the overview first.
- Type the code yourself.
- Pause after each step and verify what happened.
- Use the summary and chapter terms when you review.
- Treat confusion as information about what to check next.

## Start here

Begin with the **Preface**, then move into **Tenacity**.
